mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP above trend indicates robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is positive but the S&P 500 produces negative returns over the following 60 trading days in more than half of the quarters, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 5% signals intentional monetary tightening to combat inflation, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of FEDFUNDS threshold crossings vs 20-day forward returns is not negative at the 95% confidence level over all available history, or if positive returns occur in >40% of threshold events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment signal that consumer demand is strong and broadening into the labor market, which bolsters corporate revenue and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both increase from previous month
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces a mean 20-day forward return that is not statistically greater than zero (one-sided t-test, p > 0.05) or if the signal fails to outperform a simple single-series threshold in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate has risen by more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior 6 months, it indicates a weakening labor market that will reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.5 pct points over 6 months
failure_condition: If the signal's 60-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met, or if the hit ratio is below 55% over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and a healthy economy, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is -0.1 or weaker over a full market cycle (at least 10 years), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,500 thousand units signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day forward return following HOUST > 1500 is less than the unconditional mean or if the signal has a negative hit ratio (returns positive <50% of the time).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rate below 4% and declining unemployment rate over 3 months creates a 'goldilocks' environment of accommodative monetary policy and improving labor market, which is highly favorable for equity prices.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not yield a positive mean excess return over 20-day forward S&P 500 compared to a random draw, with at least 2% annualized outperformance, tested over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of below-trend economic growth inferred from low GDP growth, retail sales growth becomes a leading indicator of a demand recovery, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the condition of below-trend GDP growth does not increase the predictive power of RSAFS growth for 5-day forward returns, measured by a significant difference in rank IC between high- and low-growth regimes (p > 0.10).
